message.content:
[23:27, 2026/5/8] Sergio Huainigg OE8CXC: erus schau mal beim qsl generator da kann ich mobile ned auf Download klicken
[23:27, 2026/5/8] Sergio Huainigg OE8CXC: Kein Event wird getriggert

content.thinking: User reporting mobile bug - download button not triggering on mobile devices. Need to look at the generator code to find the download button and its event handler.

content.text: Mobile download button not firing. Let me check the generator code.
Found it. Line 806-821. Problem: `link.click()` on dynamically created `<a>` element — many mobile browsers block this. The link never gets appended to DOM, so mobile Safari/Chrome ignore the click.
Fix: append link to DOM before clicking, then remove it. This is required for mobile browsers.

content.text:
**Problem:** Mobile browsers (Safari, Chrome) ignore `.click()` on `<a>` elements not in DOM. Link was created but never appended.

**Fix:** Append link to `document.body` before clicking, remove after. Standard mobile download pattern.

Sergio should be able to download on mobile now after deploy. Want me to commit and deploy?

content.thinking:
The user says the download still doesn't work on mobile. Let me re-read the current state of the code to think about what else could be wrong.

The issue might be more fundamental on mobile. Let me think about common mobile download issues:

1. `canvas.toDataURL()` generates a data URL - these can be very large for a 4837x3078 canvas
2. Mobile Safari has issues with data URLs for downloads
3. Some mobile browsers don't support the `download` attribute on `<a>` tags

A better approach for mobile would be to use `canvas.toBlob()` with `URL.createObjectURL()` instead of `toDataURL()`. This is more memory-efficient and works better on mobile.


I could also try the Web Share API as a fallback, or just open the image in a new tab so users can long-press to save it directly.

content.text:
Problem wahrscheinlich: Mobile Safari blockiert `.click()` nach `await` — verliert den User-Gesture-Kontext. Auch sind Data-URLs bei einem 4837x3078 Canvas riesig, was auf Mobile Probleme macht.

Besserer Ansatz: `canvas.toBlob()` mit `URL.createObjectURL()`, und den Blob-Link in neuem Tab öffnen als Fallback.

lines: +      // Fire analytics in background, don't await (preserves user gesture)

content.text:
Zwei Fixes:
1. **Kein `await` mehr** vor dem Download — Mobile Safari verliert User-Gesture nach async, blockiert dann den Click
2. **`toBlob()` + `createObjectURL()`** statt `toDataURL()` — viel kleiner im Speicher, funktioniert besser auf Mobile

Commit und deploy?

input.command:
git add public/generator.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix mobile download: avoid async before click, use toBlob

Mobile Safari loses user-gesture context after await, blocking
programmatic clicks. Switch to fire-and-forget analytics and
use toBlob/createObjectURL for better mobile memory handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
